name: Dynamic Stretch Routine
desc: Full-body dynamic stretching sequence including leg swings, arm circles, hip rotations, and torso twists. Prepares joints and muscles for high-intensity football activity.

name: High Knees & Butt Kicks
desc: Players jog across the field alternating between high knees and butt kicks. Activates hip flexors and hamstrings while raising heart rate.

name: Lateral Shuffle Activation
desc: Players shuffle laterally across 10-yard lanes, focusing on low hips, wide base, and quick feet. Activates hip abductors and prepares for lateral movement.

name: Carioca Footwork
desc: Players perform carioca (grapevine) steps across the field, rotating hips over and under. Develops hip mobility and coordination essential for football movement patterns.

name: Backpedal & Break
desc: Players backpedal 10 yards then break hard at a 45-degree angle on coach's signal. Activates defensive back mechanics and prepares hips for change of direction.

name: Inchworm Push-Up Walk
desc: Players walk hands out to a push-up position, perform one push-up, walk feet to hands and repeat. Activates the full posterior chain and core.

name: Resistance Band Hip Activation
desc: Using bands around ankles, players perform monster walks, lateral steps, and clamshells. Activates glutes and hip stabilizers critical for explosion off the line.

name: Acceleration Build-Ups
desc: Players run 40 yards at progressive intensities — 50%, 70%, 90%, then full speed. Prepares the neuromuscular system for explosive sprinting.

name: A-Skip & B-Skip Mechanics
desc: Players perform A-skips (drive knee up, push off ground) and B-skips (extend leg forward after knee drive). Reinforces sprint mechanics and foot strike patterns.

name: Ball Handling Activation
desc: Players rotate the ball around their waist, through their legs, and in figure-8 patterns. Wakes up hand-eye coordination and ball security awareness before contact drills.

name: Partner Reaction Drill
desc: Partners face each other 2 yards apart. One player mirrors the other's movements on visual cue. Develops reaction time and first-step quickness.

name: Team Stretch Circle
desc: Full team stretches together in a circle led by a captain. Includes static quad, hamstring, groin, and shoulder stretches. Builds team cohesion and full-body readiness.

name: Stance & Start Fundamentals
desc: Players work their stance — two-point, three-point, and four-point — focusing on weight distribution, heel-to-toe balance, and first-step explosiveness off the line.

name: Footwork Ladder Sequence
desc: Agility ladder drills including single-leg hops, lateral ins-and-outs, and two-in two-out patterns. Develops foot speed and coordination for all positions.

name: Mirror Drill
desc: Two players face each other across a line. One leads, one mirrors. Develops reactive agility, lateral quickness, and hip flexibility required in man-coverage and run defense.

name: Cone Change-of-Direction
desc: 5-10-5 shuttle and L-cone drills timed individually. Measures and develops lateral quickness and the ability to decelerate and re-accelerate through direction changes.

name: Explosion Box Drill
desc: Four cones set in a square. Players sprint to each cone using different movements — forward sprint, lateral shuffle, backpedal, diagonal break. Full movement vocabulary in one drill.

name: Ball Security Gauntlet
desc: Ball carriers run through a gauntlet of coaches and players swatting at the ball. Reinforces four-point contact (fingertips, wrist, forearm, bicep) and ball protection under pressure.

name: One-on-One Competitive
desc: Individual match-ups across all positions — WR vs CB, TE vs LB, RB vs safety. Competitive reps that reveal individual technique and toughness. Film for position group review.

name: Hand Fighting Technique
desc: Offensive and defensive players practice hand placement, punch timing, and swim/rip moves in controlled partner situations. Critical for O-line/D-line and WR/CB match-ups.

name: Leverage & Pad Level
desc: Players work with sleds and shields on maintaining low pad level through contact. Emphasizes bending at the knees and driving through the point of attack.

name: Reaction Ball Drops
desc: Coach drops a reaction ball and players sprint to field it before the second bounce. Develops reaction time, first-step quickness, and ball tracking for all skill positions.

name: Route Tree Individual
desc: QBs throw to receivers running each route on the tree — out, in, curl, comeback, post, corner, go, slant, flat. Establishes route precision and QB-WR timing on all nine routes.

name: 7-on-7 vs Cover 2
desc: Offense runs scripted plays against a Cover 2 shell. Focus on identifying safety help, attacking the deep middle seam, and throwing the 5-yard out before the corner squeezes.

name: 7-on-7 vs Cover 3
desc: Offense attacks Cover 3 by targeting the curl-flat, attacking the boundary corner with fades, and hitting the deep third holes. QB reads single-high safety rotation.

name: 7-on-7 vs Man Coverage
desc: Offense runs routes against man coverage without a free safety. WRs work releases at the line of scrimmage. QBs throw back shoulder and trust the route-runner's separation.

name: Quick Game Install
desc: Focus on the slant-flat, hitch-seam, and stick-nod concepts. QB catches snap, sets feet, and delivers in 1.8 seconds or less. Develops the short passing game used against pressure.

name: Drop-Back Mechanics
desc: QBs work 3-step, 5-step, and 7-step drops without receivers. Focus on footwork, shoulder turn, and platform readiness at the top of each drop. Filmed for individual review.

name: Pocket Movement Drill
desc: QB works pocket movement — climb the pocket, slide left/right — while reading a downfield target held by a coach. Develops the ability to throw accurately while moving within structure.

name: Bootleg & Rollout Package
desc: QB executes play-action boots and sprint-outs to both sides. Receivers run defined rollout routes. Focus on QB hitching up his momentum before throwing across his body.

name: Mesh Concept Install
desc: Installs the mesh crossing concept — two shallow crossers rubbing at the mesh point with a wheel route behind. QB reads underneath to wheel based on linebacker drops.

name: Four Verticals
desc: All four receivers run vertical routes. QB reads the coverage and targets the open seam — inside releases vs Cover 2, outside vs Cover 3. Stretches the defense vertically.

name: Scramble Drill
desc: QB drops back and a coach signals scramble. Receivers break off their routes to find open grass while QB extends the play. Develops improvisation and off-schedule football.

name: Red Zone Pass Concepts
desc: Run compressed route combinations inside the 10 — fades, corners, back-of-the-end-zone digs. Defense plays tight man. Focus on back-shoulder throws and high-point catches.

name: WR Release vs Press
desc: WRs work against press coverage — stem, jab step, and release technique. Outside release, inside release, and speed release practiced one-on-one against a corner.

name: Air Raid Mesh & Spot
desc: Run the air-raid staples — mesh, stick, and Y-cross — against a scout defense at tempo. Ball is out in 2.5 seconds or less on every rep.

name: RPO Installation
desc: QB reads a pre-snap key and post-snap gives or pulls based on that read. Bubble screen, slant, and quick out attached to inside zone. Develops dual-threat QB instincts.

name: Inside Zone Install
desc: Offense installs inside zone with full O-line. RB reads the backside guard, finds the cutback or keeps the playside crease. Emphasis on combo blocks working to the second level.

name: Outside Zone / Stretch
desc: O-line and RB run the stretch play to both sides. RB aims for the outside hip of the TE and cuts back across the grain when the corner closes. Develops vision and patience.

name: Power Run Install
desc: Installs power — FB or TE kicks out the EMOL while the backside guard pulls and leads through the hole. RB runs off the lead block. Fundamental gap-scheme introduction.

name: Counter Trey
desc: Guard and tackle pull backside while the RB takes a false-step to freeze linebackers. Develops misdirection timing between the handoff and the RB's counter step.

name: Draw Play
desc: QB fakes a pass drop, hands to RB running a draw path. O-line sets pass protection then releases to second level. Develops deception timing and patience from the RB.

name: QB Keeper & Designed Run
desc: Mobile QB runs designed keepers, sneaks, and zone-read pulls. Focus on cut decision, shoulder pad lowering at contact, and protecting the football through the tackle.

name: RB Vision Cone Drill
desc: RB runs through cones simulating an O-line block sequence, reading a cut lane and hitting it at full speed. Develops patience behind the line and burst through the hole.

name: Stiff Arm & Jump Cut
desc: RB runs through a course with defenders positioned to tackle. Work the stiff arm, jump cut, and spin move in succession. Develops elusiveness after contact.

name: Toss Sweep
desc: QB tosses the ball to an RB sweeping the edge. WR cracks inside, FB kicks out the corner. Timing of the toss, arc of the RB, and WR crack timing all practiced in sequence.

name: Goal Line Run Package
desc: Run goal line formations from the 1- and 2-yard lines — QB sneak, fullback dive, off-tackle power. Physical reps with emphasis on low pad level and finishing through contact.

name: Screen Game Install
desc: Installs RB screen, middle screen, and tunnel screen. QB drops deep and throws back across the LOS. O-line releases to set blocks. RB reads the blocks downfield.

name: Zone Read Option
desc: QB reads the unblocked defensive end — if he crashes give to RB, if he stays pull and keep. Live reps with a scout DE making a decision. Develops QB mesh-point patience.

name: Hands Catching Technique
desc: Receivers catch 100 balls thrown to all catch windows — high, low, left, right, behind the head. Focus on soft hands, eyes tracking the ball to the tuck, and no body-catching.

name: Catch & Run After Catch
desc: WRs catch a pass then immediately work run-after-catch — turn upfield, avoid cones simulating defenders, finish to the end zone. Develops YAC instincts.

name: Contested Catch Drills
desc: WR and CB compete for 50/50 balls thrown to the boundary and end zone corners. WR works high-point, body positioning, and physicality through the catch.

name: Slant Route Timing
desc: WRs run slant routes at precise depths while QB drops three steps and fires on the break. Timing is everything — no double-catches.

name: Comeback & Out Route Precision
desc: Receivers run comebacks (14-16 yards) and out routes (6-8 yards) with emphasis on a clean break — no drift, sharp cut. QB delivers before the receiver comes out of his break.

name: TE Seam Route
desc: Tight ends run seam routes down the hash, working to split the safeties in Cover 2 or beat a linebacker over the top in Cover 1. Focus on releasing off the line and accelerating through.

name: RB Check-Down & Wheel Route
desc: RBs work the check-down catch in the flat and the wheel route — releasing out of the backfield, running the flat, then bending upfield. Timing coordinated with the QB.

name: Boundary Fade Route
desc: WR and QB work the boundary fade — WR presses inside, fades to the back pylon, QB lofts the ball to the back shoulder. Critical red-zone connection requiring precise ball placement.

name: Catch in Traffic
desc: Receiver runs a crossing route through a zone of defenders with pool noodles or bags. Must catch the ball cleanly while absorbing simulated contact mid-route. Builds courage over the middle.

name: Dig & Post Corner Route
desc: Receivers work the dig (in route at 12-15 yards) and post-corner combination. QB reads the safety — if safety drives on the post, throw the corner behind him.

name: WR Blocking Technique
desc: Receivers work crack blocks, stalk blocks on corners, and downfield seal blocks on linebackers. Perimeter blocking is often the difference in the run game.

name: Stance & First Step
desc: O-linemen work stance fundamentals — feet width, weight in balls of feet, flat back — then fire out on snap count with a 6-inch first step. Timed from snap to contact.

name: Hand Placement & Punch
desc: Linemen work hand placement on shield bags — thumbs up, inside the frame, elbows in — delivering a powerful punch that locks out the defender. Focus on timing and extension.

name: Double-Team Block
desc: Two linemen combo block a single defender — one takes the post, one takes the lead — working together to drive the defender off the ball, then one releases to the second level.

name: Pass Protection Mirror
desc: O-linemen set pass protection against a live rusher using mirror technique — kick-slide, maintain inside leverage, punch on contact. Focus on footwork and patience.

name: Pull Technique
desc: Guards and tackles work pull mechanics — open pivot step, flat pull path down the line, kick-out block on the EMOL or log block into the hole. Timed and filmed for review.

name: Zone Blocking Steps
desc: Full O-line practices zone blocking footwork — lateral first step to the playside, combo block with adjacent lineman, and climb to the linebacker. Run both inside and outside zone.

name: Reach Block
desc: Linemen practice reach blocks — getting outside leverage on a defender who is head-up or shaded away. Emphasis on the flat lateral first step and winning the hand fight outside.

name: Pass Set vs Speed Rush
desc: O-linemen face a speed-rush defensive end. Work the quick kick-slide, redirect laterally, and mirror the edge rusher without giving ground. Develops lateral pass-set quickness.

name: Chute & Sled Drive
desc: Linemen fire out through a blocking chute (forces low pad level) then drive a 7-man sled for 5 yards. Reinforces staying low and maintaining leg drive through the point of attack.

name: 5-Man Unit Communication
desc: O-line calls out protections together against various defensive alignments — 4-3, 3-4, nickel, overload blitz. Builds cohesion and pre-snap communication habits.

name: Get-Off & First Step Explosion
desc: D-linemen work off the line on a ball snap, driving through a bag on their first step. Emphasis on anticipating the snap count and getting off the ball as fast as legally possible.

name: Rip & Swim Pass Rush
desc: D-linemen work rip (under the arm) and swim (over the arm) pass-rush moves against a partner or shield bag. Emphasis on timing the move off the blocker's punch.

name: Bull Rush & Anchor
desc: Defensive tackles and ends bull rush a 2-man sled for 5 yards — drive through contact, maintain leverage, and anchor at the point of attack. Builds base strength and leg drive.

name: Counter Spin Move
desc: D-lineman sets up an outside rush, then counters with an inside spin move when the blocker oversets. Work the full sequence — setup, spin, burst to the QB — at game speed.

name: Run Fits & Gap Responsibility
desc: D-line works their gap assignments against a run-heavy scout offense. Each lineman is responsible for their gap and must not allow cutback by over-pursuing.

name: Tackle & Wrap Technique
desc: D-linemen practice open-field tackling — break down, eyes up, drive through the ball carrier with both arms wrapping, and drive the feet through contact. Finish every rep.

name: 3-Move Pass Rush Plan
desc: Each D-lineman works a 3-move pass-rush plan — primary move, counter, and counter to the counter. Partner resistance at half-speed to perfect technique before live reps.

name: Slant & Stunt Execution
desc: D-line runs T-T games and E-T stunts against a live O-line — one lineman occupies two blockers while the other crashes the gap. Timing and communication are critical.

name: Setting the Edge
desc: Defensive ends practice setting the edge against outside zone and toss runs — maintain outside leverage, squeeze the blocker inside, force the ball carrier to cut back to pursuit.

name: Block Destruction
desc: D-linemen practice defeating specific blocks — double teams, chip blocks from TEs, and reach blocks from guards. Work shedding each block type and redirecting to the ball.

name: Drop & Zone Coverage
desc: Linebackers drop into hook-curl, flat, and hook-to-curl zones on the snap. Read QB eyes, break on the ball's flight, and locate the receiver before making the tackle.

name: Man Coverage on RB & TE
desc: LBs cover RBs and TEs in man coverage out of the backfield and off the line. Work the release — bump and redirect at the LOS — then turn and run with the receiver.

name: Run-Pass Conflict Read
desc: LBs read the offensive tackle's block to determine run or pass. If tackle blocks down, flow to the run. If tackle sets pass protection, drop into coverage.

name: Blitz Package Install
desc: Install a variety of blitz packages — A-gap pressure, weak-side edge blitz, and zone blitz. LBs learn their assignment, timing off the snap, and how to convert if blocked.

name: Scrape Exchange
desc: DE crashes inside while LB scrapes around the edge on zone-read plays. Timing of the scrape, exchange of gap responsibilities, and proper LB path to the corner are emphasized.

name: Defeat the Block
desc: LBs work shedding offensive linemen attacking them in the second level — rip through, swim over, and press-and-shed techniques practiced against live blockers at half speed.

name: Angle Tackle Drill
desc: LBs practice angle tackling — approach from the side, break down at 2 yards, drive shoulder through the near hip, wrap arms and finish. Critical for open-field stops.

name: Fast Flow Pursuit
desc: LBs read the flow of the play and pursue to the football at full speed across multiple run scenarios — inside zone, outside zone, counter. Take correct pursuit angles to the ball.

name: Screen Recognition
desc: LBs read the screen — offensive linemen releasing, QB setting up deep — and immediately redirect to the flat to disrupt the screen at or behind the line of scrimmage.

name: Stack & Fill Technique
desc: ILBs work staying clean behind the D-line by stacking blocks, then filling the correct gap once the running lane is defined. Patience and instincts developed together.

name: Backpedal & Break Technique
desc: DBs work their backpedal — high hips, heel-to-toe roll, shoulders back — then break at 45 degrees on a coach's signal. Fundamental coverage skill drilled in isolation.

name: Press Coverage Technique
desc: CBs work press alignment — inside shade, punch the receiver's release point, and redirect inside or force outside before turning to run. Paired one-on-one with WRs at the line.

name: Zone Drop & Find the Ball
desc: DBs drop into their zone assignments, keep eyes on the QB through their drop, and break on the throw. Work all three levels — flat, hook, and deep third.

name: Bail Technique (Cover 3 CB)
desc: Boundary corner bails at the snap — opens hips, gains depth, and flies to the deep third. Work against receivers running double moves; CB must not bite on the first cut.

name: Safety Run Fill
desc: Safeties rotate down on run support — read the block of the TE or H-back, fill the edge gap, and make the open-field tackle. Emphasis on decisive trigger and safe approach angle.

name: Half-Field Coverage Drill
desc: Strong safety and corner work half-field coverage together — corner takes deep half, safety plays the flat-to-curl. Receivers run combination routes to stress the coverage.

name: Pick-Six Intercept Drill
desc: DBs track underthrown or batted balls — turn and run with the ball in the air, catch at the highest point, and score. Reinforces return instincts after the interception.

name: Trail Technique in Man
desc: DB trails the receiver at hip level in man coverage — inside leverage, one arm's length, eyes on the receiver's numbers until the ball is in the air, then find and play the ball.

name: Safety Communication
desc: Free and strong safety align together and communicate coverage calls, rotation checks, and alert calls before and at the snap. Builds the communication habit essential at every level.

name: Bump & Run Gauntlet
desc: CB runs the gauntlet — press the first receiver, release, sprint to the next WR, bump, and repeat. Builds the explosive change-of-direction needed to maintain press coverage.

name: Post Safety Help
desc: Free safety works the post rotation — reads the post route threat, rotates over the top, and arrives at the deep middle hash in time to contest the catch or make the tackle.

name: Kickoff Coverage Lanes
desc: 11-man kickoff unit practices lane discipline — each player maintains their lane, avoids the wedge, and converges on the returner. Film to check containment and lane integrity.

name: Kickoff Return Blocks
desc: Return team installs the kickoff return scheme — wedge formation, up-man assignments, and the returner's cut lane. Work both middle and sideline return calls.

name: Punt Coverage
desc: Punt coverage team works their release off the line — gunners beat jams, personal protectors support, and coverage converges on the returner without over-running the ball.

name: Punt Return Scheme
desc: Install the punt return — fair-catch priority, wall return left/right, or safe return middle. Returner catches punts under pressure while the front 8 set the return wall.

name: PAT & Field Goal Protection
desc: Kicking team installs PAT and field goal protection — line splits, personal protector depth, and snapper-holder-kicker timing. Work at full speed with a scout rush.

name: Field Goal Block Package
desc: Defense installs a field goal block package — penetration lanes, timing off the snap, and hand-up technique at the kick point. One block changes a game.

name: Onside Kick Execution
desc: Kicking team practices the onside kick — directional kick, recovery alignment, and execution timing. Also install the hands team on the receiving end.

name: Fake Punt Install
desc: Install one or two fake punt plays — direct snap to an up-back, punter pass to the gunner, or spread punt with a designed run. Run against a full punt return team.

name: Snapper Accuracy
desc: Long and short snapper work isolated reps — 15-yard snap to punter, 7-yard snap to holder. Timed and filmed. Work spiral rotation and target accuracy.

name: Gunner Release Drill
desc: Punt gunners work releasing off a jammer at the line — speed release, inside release, and double-move release. Get off the line fast and run to the ball.

name: Coffin Corner Punt
desc: Punter works directional kicking targeting the coffin corner inside the 10-yard line. Focus on hang time, directional accuracy, and making the returner field under pressure.

name: Inside-the-10 Pass Concepts
desc: Run compressed route combinations inside the 10 — fade-stop, corner-flat, back-shoulder throw. Field is shortened; every route must be tighter and more precise.

name: Goal Line Run Package
desc: Install goal line formations — QB sneak, jumbo package power, off-tackle, and naked bootleg. Physical reps with full line contact. Emphasis on winning the leverage battle.

name: Red Zone Defense Cover 0
desc: Defense plays Cover 0 man with no free safety. DBs must cover with no help over the top. LBs match any eligible receiver out of the backfield. High-intensity competitive reps.

name: Red Zone 7-on-7
desc: Offense runs red zone pass concepts vs. defense's red zone coverage shells — alternating Cover 2, Cover 0, and zone blitz. Score or stop is the only outcome.

name: 2-Point Conversion Package
desc: Install 2-point conversion plays from the 3-yard line — quick slant, QB sneak, designed run, and play-action fade. Defense plays live. Outcome tracked for game planning.

name: 4th-Down Package
desc: Install the 4th-and-short and 4th-and-goal package — power, QB sneak, and a short pass concept. Offense and defense compete live. Builds clutch execution habits.

name: Red Zone Run Fits
desc: Defense works gap assignments and run fits inside the 20 where field space is compressed. Linebackers must fit quickly with no pursuit angle available.

name: Red Zone Situational Script
desc: Run a full scripted red zone period — 1st-and-goal, 2nd-and-goal, 3rd-and-short, 4th-and-goal. Both units compete with downs and distance tracked. Film and review.

name: Two-Minute Offense Install
desc: Install the two-minute offense — no-huddle operation, spike play, sideline routes, and clock management. QB must communicate at the line of scrimmage and get the ball snapped with urgency.

name: Two-Minute Defense
desc: Defense practices the two-minute drill — zone prevent, don't give up the sideline, allow only short completions. Tackling in bounds is the priority. Clock management is everything.

name: Clock Management Scenarios
desc: Offense and defense work specific clock scenarios — 90 seconds no timeouts, 45 seconds two timeouts, final spike play. Make decisions in real time under simulated pressure.

name: Hurry-Up No-Huddle
desc: Offense operates the full hurry-up no-huddle at game speed — signal system from the sideline, alignment adjustments at the line, and quick snap execution.

name: Spike Play Execution
desc: QB and offense practice the spike play — fast break from the previous play, get set, and snap-and-spike in under 3 seconds. Timing between the QB and offensive line is critical.

name: Final Drive Simulation
desc: Simulate a full final drive — offense starts at own 25 with 1:45 and one timeout, needs a field goal to win. Defense plays live. Tracks real time, real downs, real decisions.

name: 11-on-11 Full Scrimmage
desc: Full team scrimmage with live contact. Run from a full script — run and pass plays on offense, base defense with all fronts and coverages. Film every series for review.

name: Thud Scrimmage
desc: Full team live reps at thud contact — full speed to the tackle, contact is controlled (no takedowns). Reduces injury risk while maintaining competitive execution at practice speed.

name: Situational Scrimmage
desc: Scripted situation scrimmage — 3rd-and-short, 3rd-and-long, red zone, two-minute — run in sequence. Both units compete for results. Builds situational awareness and execution.

name: Scout Team vs. Starters
desc: Scout team runs the opponent's base offense and defense against the starting units. Prepares the team for specific tendencies, formations, and plays they will see on game day.

name: Game Plan Install Period
desc: Walk through the week's game plan at full speed. Every play installed against live opposition. Focus is on execution of the new install, not winning competitive reps.

name: Turnover Circuit Scrimmage
desc: Defense earns extra plays for turnovers — every pick or fumble recovery earns a fresh drive. Rewards defensive playmaking and instills a turnover mindset on both sides of the ball.

name: 110s (Gassers)
desc: Players sprint 110 yards (sideline to sideline and back), rest 30 seconds, repeat 6-10 times. Builds lactate threshold and mental toughness.

name: Position 40-Yard Dash Sets
desc: Players run position-specific 40-yard dashes with timed rest between reps. Linemen run every 90 seconds; skill players every 60 seconds. Tracked and compared weekly.

name: Pursuit Drill
desc: All 11 defensive players sprint to the ball from their assigned positions on a play call — even if the ball is on the opposite side. Develops pursuit habit and full-team conditioning.

name: Oklahoma Drill
desc: One-on-one — blocker and defender in a confined lane with a ball carrier. Physical, competitive reps that test toughness, leverage, and contact conditioning. Rotate all positions.

name: Up-Downs
desc: Players jog in place then drop to the ground and jump back up as fast as possible on coach's signal. Work in 30-second intervals. Develops full-body explosiveness and conditioning.

name: Bear Crawl & Crab Walk
desc: Players bear crawl 10 yards and crab walk back for multiple rounds. Develops functional core strength, shoulder stability, and conditioning that translates to low-pad-level play.

name: Metabolic Agility Circuit
desc: Four-station circuit: sprint 20 yards, 10 push-ups, lateral shuffle 10 yards each way, and 5 squat jumps. 45 seconds on, 15 off for three rounds. Full-body metabolic conditioning.

name: Position Conditioning Finish
desc: End practice with position-specific conditioning — linemen run 10-yard short sprints; skill players run routes for conditioning. Everyone finishes together as a team.

name: Mat Drills
desc: Team works a sequence of mat drills — barrel rolls, forward-backward rolls, and fireman carries. Builds full-body strength, coordination, and mental toughness through fatigue.

name: Sprint-to-Jog Intervals
desc: Players alternate 20-yard sprints with 20-yard jogs for 8-12 intervals. Mimics the work-rest ratio of football plays — explosive effort followed by brief recovery before going again.

name: Captain's Huddle
desc: Team captains lead a brief meeting at midfield — address the team on effort, culture, and the week's theme. Builds leadership habits and gives captains a platform before the team.

name: Compete Card Recognition
desc: Coaches recognize players who earned compete cards during the week for exceptional effort or improvement. Public recognition in front of the team builds a culture of accountability.

name: Unit Challenge Competition
desc: Offense vs. defense in a non-football challenge — tug-of-war, relay race, or trivia. Builds team cohesion and competitive spirit in a fun setting outside of scheme.

name: Film Room Accountability
desc: Team reviews the best and worst execution from the previous game on a projected screen on the field. Accountability is public and immediate — builds a film culture across the roster.

name: Post-Practice Bring-It-In
desc: Full team gathers at midfield after practice. Captains lead the break — a word, a chant, or a commitment. Ends every practice with unity and sets the tone for the next session.

name: Water Break
desc: Scheduled hydration break with players at their position group stations. Coaches use this time to give brief individual corrections while players hydrate and recover between periods.

name: Equipment Check Break
desc: Players check and adjust pads, helmets, and cleats. Trainers circulate to address minor tape jobs or equipment issues before returning to full-speed practice.

name: Transition Between Periods
desc: Planned transition time between major practice periods. Coaches set up the next drill while players move to their next formation. Builds efficient practice habits.

name: Foam Roll Recovery Prep
desc: Players use foam rollers on quads, IT bands, calves, and thoracic spine before practice. Releases myofascial tension and improves range of motion prior to dynamic activity.

name: Sprint Mechanic Drills
desc: Wall drills and falling starts to reinforce proper sprint posture — shin angle, arm drive, and triple extension. Run through three progressions before transitioning to full-speed work.

name: Jump Rope Activation
desc: Players jump rope for 3-5 minutes using single jumps, double-unders, and alternating feet. Elevates heart rate and develops the ankle stiffness and foot speed needed for explosiveness.

name: Scorpion & Iron Cross
desc: Players lie on their backs and perform iron cross hip rotations, then flip prone for scorpion stretches. Mobilizes the thoracic spine, hip flexors, and glutes before contact work.

name: Lunge Matrix
desc: Forward lunge, lateral lunge, and rotational lunge performed across 10 yards in sequence. Opens the hips through all three planes of movement and activates the glutes and adductors.

name: Ankle Mobility Circuit
desc: Players work ankle circles, heel-toe walks, and banded ankle dorsiflexion against a cone. Improves ankle mobility critical for cut-and-go movements and low pad level.

name: Shoulder Circle & Band Pull-Aparts
desc: Arm circles progressing to large, band pull-aparts, and overhead reaches. Activates the rotator cuff and shoulder stabilizers before throwing and blocking activities.

name: Zig-Zag Cone Run
desc: Players weave through 8 cones set 3 yards apart at a 45-degree angle. Focus on planting the outside foot hard on each cut and driving out of the break at full speed.

name: Broad Jump Activation
desc: Three consecutive two-foot broad jumps emphasizing maximum horizontal distance. Land softly on both feet with knees bent and hips back. Activates posterior chain and triple extension.

name: T-Drill Warm-Up
desc: Run the T-drill at 70% intensity as a warm-up movement pattern. Focus on the lateral shuffle mechanics and the backpedal return rather than maximum time.

name: Medicine Ball Overhead Toss
desc: Partners toss a medicine ball overhead back and forth from 5 yards. Activates the core, shoulders, and posterior chain while warming up the explosive power needed for blocking and tackling.

name: Glute Bridge Activation
desc: Players perform two-leg and single-leg glute bridges, holding the top position for 2 counts. Activates the glutes and hamstrings before any sprint or contact work.

name: Hip Flexor Lunge & Reach
desc: Players step into a deep lunge and reach the opposite hand toward the sky, rotating the trunk. Progressively opens the hip flexors and thoracic rotation to full range.

name: Defensive Back Hip Circles
desc: DBs and skill players perform standing hip circles — both directions, progressing to knee-drive hip swings. Targets the hip rotators critical for defensive back footwork and transitions.

name: Controlled Sprint Buildup
desc: Three progressive sprint sets at 60, 80, and 95 percent over 30 yards. Full recovery between each. Final rep is a flying 10-yard burst. Prepares the nervous system for full-speed play.

name: Reactive Start Drill
desc: Players in a two-point stance react to a visual cue (coach hand drop) and sprint 5 yards. Develops the reactive start critical for all positions on both sides of the ball.

name: Bear Crawl Warm-Up
desc: Players bear crawl 10 yards forward and reverse for two rounds. Warms up the core, shoulders, and hips while reinforcing the low body position needed for contact football.

name: Power Skip for Distance
desc: Players perform maximum-distance power skips across 20 yards. Drives single-leg explosiveness, arm drive coordination, and hip extension mechanics.

name: Transverse Lunge & Twist
desc: Players step at 135 degrees, lower into a lunge, and rotate the torso toward the lead knee. Works the rotational mobility and hip stability needed for block-and-shed movements.

name: Low Shuffle to Sprint
desc: Players lateral shuffle 5 yards in a low athletic stance then fire upfield on the coach's signal. Combines lateral activation with linear acceleration in a single warm-up rep.

name: Two-Point Stance Starts
desc: Skill players take a two-point stance and fire out on cadence. Focus on a short, powerful first step, low lean angle, and driving into the first three steps at maximum effort.

name: Downhill Run Technique
desc: Players run down a slight grade (or simulate it) at controlled speed, focusing on forward lean, short quick steps, and high knees. Reinforces downhill running mechanics for RBs and returners.

name: Wrist & Elbow Activation
desc: Players rotate wrists, squeeze a stress ball, and perform light elbow circles. Essential warm-up for QBs, WRs, OL, and DL who depend on hand and arm strength through contact.

name: Partner Band Resisted Shuffle
desc: One player loops a resistance band around the waist of their partner and applies light resistance during a lateral shuffle. Warms the hip abductors and reinforces lateral push-off mechanics.

name: Neck Strengthening Circuit
desc: Players perform neck bridges, isometric resistance holds, and neck circles in all planes. Neck strength is a critical injury prevention tool for every position in contact football.

name: Jump & Stick Landing
desc: Players jump vertically or laterally and stick the landing in a single-leg athletic position, holding for 2 seconds. Trains landing mechanics and single-leg stability before live contact.

name: Lateral Band Walk
desc: Bands around ankles, players walk laterally 10 yards each direction maintaining a squat position. Isolates the hip abductors and activates the glutes before agility and cutting drills.

name: Full-Team Run-Through
desc: All 22 players run through three plays at walk-then-jog-then-sprint pace — one run, one pass, one special teams look. Final step before live reps; aligns the team on alignment and assignment.

name: Hurdle Mobility Sequence
desc: Mini hurdles set at shin height. Players step over and under the hurdles laterally, forward, and backward. Develops hip mobility and spatial body awareness in all directions.

name: Rotational Medicine Ball Slam
desc: Players rotate and slam a medicine ball into the ground from both sides. Warms the obliques, shoulders, and hips through the rotational pattern used in blocking and tackling.

name: Standing Hip CARs
desc: Controlled articular rotations of the hip joint — full circle, each direction, single leg. Primes the hip joint through its full range before any explosive or contact movement.

name: Rapid Fire Footwork Ladder
desc: High-speed agility ladder work — icky shuffle, Ickey shuffle, and lateral two-in/two-out. Timed for competitive motivation. Develops foot speed and rhythm before positional drills.

name: Jog-to-Cut Activation
desc: Players jog at 50 percent then cut hard left or right on a coach's whistle. No warning — pure reaction. Warms up the stop-and-redirect movement pattern used on every play.

name: Defensive Line Pad Warm-Up
desc: D-linemen work a light bag punch sequence — hand placement, recoil, and reset — before live reps. Activates the hand-eye coordination and upper-body engagement of the pass rush.

name: Position Group Activation Period
desc: Position groups split for group-specific activation — OL fire-out bags, DBs backpedal, WRs route stems, LBs drops. Primes each unit for their specific movement demands.

name: Sprinter Starts
desc: Players assume a sprinter stance — one hand down, back foot up — and fire out on the cadence signal. Three reps each side. Develops explosive first-step mechanics from a low starting position.

name: Full-Body PVC Mobility
desc: Players use PVC pipes for overhead squats, thoracic rotations, and hip hinges. Identifies mobility restrictions and reinforces full range of motion patterns before heavy contact work.

name: Sideline-to-Sideline Activation
desc: Players run sideline to sideline mixing movements — sprint, shuffle, backpedal, carioca — changing on the coach's command. Final full-field warm-up before scripted practice begins.

name: L-Cone Drill
desc: Players run the L-cone (3-cone drill) — sprint 5 yards, back 5 yards, figure-8 around the middle cone, and finish. Develops the tight-space change of direction critical for all skill positions.

name: Pro Agility Shuttle
desc: 5-10-5 shuttle timed individually. Players start from a two-point stance, sprint 5 yards right, 10 yards left, and 5 yards back through the start. Timed and tracked weekly for improvement.

name: Hip Sink & Redirect
desc: Players sprint toward a cone, sink the hips to decelerate, and redirect at a right angle on a visual cue. Teaches proper deceleration mechanics before changing direction at speed.

name: Stance Variation Work
desc: All positions practice their stance — two-point for skill players, three-point for linemen, two-point open for DBs. Coaches check foot width, weight distribution, and pad level on every rep.

name: Speed Turn Drill
desc: Player sprints 5 yards, executes a speed turn (180 degrees), and sprints back. Focus on the flip-the-hips technique — open the inside foot and drive out of the turn without losing speed.

name: Reactive Shuffle & Sprint
desc: Player shuffles laterally until a coach signals left or right, at which point they plant and sprint 10 yards in that direction. Develops reactive agility and hip-opening mechanics.

name: Box Shuffle Circuit
desc: Four cones in a 5-yard square. Players shuffle around the outside, touching each cone with the near hand. Progress from slow to full speed while maintaining low hips throughout.

name: Vertical Jump & Land
desc: Players perform three max vertical jumps, focusing on full triple extension at takeoff and a controlled two-foot landing. Develops explosive power and safe landing mechanics.

name: Single-Leg Stability Holds
desc: Players hold a single-leg balance on each foot for 30-second intervals, progressing to eyes closed. Develops ankle stability and proprioception critical for cutting and landing.

name: Defensive Footwork Crossover
desc: DBs and LBs work crossover steps — pivot on the back foot, crossover with the front foot, and accelerate. Practiced in both directions. Develops hip-opening speed in coverage.

name: Open-Field Tackle Breakdown
desc: Defender approaches a ball carrier in space, breaks down at 2 yards — feet chopping, hips low — and drives through the target. Full wrap technique with driving feet through contact.

name: Scramble Recovery Drill
desc: Player falls to the ground on coach's signal, gets up immediately, and sprints 5 yards. Develops the recovery ability needed after cut blocks, stumbles, or broken-play situations.

name: Pass Rush Entry Angle
desc: D-linemen and edge players work the correct path to the QB — aim for the far upfield shoulder, take a curved rush path, and flatten to the quarterback. Avoid the straight-line rush.

name: Two-Man Release Drill
desc: Offensive players work releasing from a double team or chip block — punch off the second blocker, redirect, and accelerate to the route or gap. Simulates real-game release scenarios.

name: Ball Strip Technique
desc: Defenders work ripping the ball out from behind — wrap the ball carrier, punch at the ball with the top hand, and rip with the bottom hand. Proper strip mechanics practiced 20+ reps.

name: Deceleration & Plant Drill
desc: Players sprint at full speed toward a line and decelerate over exactly 3 yards, planting hard without stumbling. Develops the braking mechanics essential for route breaks and coverage.

name: Explosive Start Variations
desc: Players practice starts from multiple positions — seated, lying on back, lying face-down, and on one knee. React to a signal and sprint 10 yards. Develops reactive explosiveness from any position.

name: Lateral Bound Series
desc: Single-leg lateral bounds across a 10-yard lane, alternating legs. Develops lateral power and single-leg landing mechanics used in cuts, shuffles, and lateral pursuit.

name: Contact Balance Drill
desc: Player runs through a gauntlet of shoulder pads pressed against them from the sides while maintaining their path and balance. Develops contact balance critical for ball carriers and blockers.

name: Pursuit Angle Simulation
desc: Defender reads a ball carrier who is running a diagonal path and sprints to intercept — not to where the carrier is, but where they will be. Develops correct pursuit angle calculation.

name: Low-to-High Explosion Drill
desc: Players start in a deep squat position, fire out to a standing position, and drive through a blocking bag. Develops the low-to-high explosion needed in run blocking and pass rush.

name: Hand Combat Circuit
desc: Partner drill cycling through punch, rip, swim, and club-and-rip hand fighting sequences. Each player alternates offense and defense. Develops hand quickness and counter-move instincts.

name: Escape & Disengage
desc: Player in a locked-up hand-fighting position with a partner works to disengage — violent rip, wrist snap, and redirect. Practiced by all positions — linemen, LBs, DBs, and WRs.

name: Reading Keys Drill
desc: Player reads a key — offensive tackle, guard, or pulling lineman — and reacts accordingly. Defenders practice reading run or pass on the first step, then executing their assignment.

name: First Contact & Separation
desc: Player makes initial contact on a blocker or defender, then separates immediately and redirects to a new target. Simulates the half-count engagement common in every football play.

name: Cone Grid Reaction Drill
desc: 12 cones in a 4x3 grid. Coach calls a cone number and player sprints to it, returns to center, and awaits the next call. Develops reactive footwork and multi-directional change of direction.

name: Shuffle to Hip-Turn Sprint
desc: Player shuffles 5 yards, opens hips and transitions to a full sprint on the signal. Develops the smooth hip-turn transition used by DBs in man coverage and by linebackers in zone.

name: Tackling Circuit
desc: Three-station circuit: angle tackles on a bag, form tackle on a dummy, and wrap-and-drive against a partner in a controlled setting. Cycles all defensive players through proper tackle technique.

name: Power Clean Position Drill
desc: Without weights, players practice the hip hinge, pull, and catch positions of a power clean. Reinforces the triple-extension mechanics and athletic catch position critical for explosive play.

name: Sprint Float Sprint
desc: 20-yard sprint, float (decelerate without stopping) for 10 yards, then re-accelerate for another 20. Develops the ability to accelerate after a broken play or change in game speed.

name: Catch & Protect Drill
desc: Player catches a ball and immediately tucks it in secure ball-carry position while a defender applies pressure. Combines receiving and ball security in a single competitive rep.

name: Read & React Keys
desc: A live offensive player takes one of three actions — run block, pass set, or pull — and the defender reacts with the correct assignment. Develops pre-snap and post-snap key reading for all defenders.

name: Footfire & Freeze Drill
desc: Players rapid-fire feet in place on the whistle and freeze immediately on a second whistle. Develops foot speed, body control, and the stop-and-reset quickness needed in all coverage scenarios.

name: Position Shadow Drill
desc: One player mirrors all movements of a partner — every cut, shuffle, and sprint replicated in real time. Develops reactive first-step quickness and the defensive mirror skills needed in coverage.

name: Juke & Hesitation Move
desc: Ball carriers work juke, hesitation, and stutter-step moves against a defender who must commit. Work left and right jukes, inside and outside hesitations. Develops elusiveness in space.

name: Hip Rotation Power Drill
desc: Players stand in an athletic stance and explosively rotate the hips to both sides using a resistance band. Develops the rotational hip power used in throwing, blocking, tackling, and cutting.

name: Defensive Press & Redirect
desc: Defender presses the offensive player at the line of scrimmage, disrupts the release, then redirects their own path into proper coverage position. Full sequence at game speed.

name: Individual Film Review
desc: Players review their own individual position film from the previous game, identifying one technique improvement and one positive. Self-evaluation develops football IQ and ownership of performance.

name: Competitive One-on-One Circuit
desc: Rotating one-on-one circuit with win-loss tracking — WR vs. CB, RB vs. LB, TE vs. S. Every player competes at least 4 reps. Leaderboard posted. Builds competitive culture across the roster.

name: Speed Ladder Complex
desc: Three-exercise ladder complex: single-leg in-out, lateral high knees, and quick feet two-in/two-out. Run each pattern twice before rotating. Develops overall foot speed and coordination.

name: Diagonal Sprint Patterns
desc: Cones set in a diamond pattern. Players sprint to each cone along diagonal paths, touching the cone and redirecting. Develops multi-directional speed and cut angles needed across all positions.

name: Pad Level War
desc: Two players compete hand-to-hand from a low stance, each trying to raise the other's pad level. Lower man wins. Teaches leverage and pad level through direct competitive pressure.

name: Catch Point Drill
desc: Players practice catching the ball at various catch points — overhead, chest, low, and behind — thrown by a JUGS machine or coach. No body-catching allowed; hands only on every rep.

name: Recovery Sprint Drill
desc: Player simulates getting beaten on a play, then pursues and closes the gap over 20 yards at maximum effort. Develops the never-quit pursuit habit and recovery speed critical in game situations.

name: Pre-Snap Alignment Check
desc: All 11 players align for a play call, then freeze. Coaches check every player's alignment, splits, and depth against the play call. No movement until every player is correctly aligned.

name: Plyometric Box Drill
desc: Players perform box jumps, lateral box jumps, and step-up explosions on a 24-inch box. Develops lower-body power and the hip-drive mechanics used in every explosive football movement.

name: Balance Beam Footwork
desc: Players walk, shuffle, and perform quick feet on a balance beam or rope laid flat. Develops proprioception and foot precision needed for cut accuracy and landing stability.

name: Tennis Ball Reaction Drops
desc: Coach holds two tennis balls at shoulder height. Drops one without warning. Player must catch it before the second bounce. Develops reaction time, first-step quickness, and hand-eye coordination.

name: Competitive Stance-Off
desc: Two players get in their respective stances facing each other. On the snap, each fires out to make contact. Lower man and better pad angle wins. A simple competitive rep to reinforce leverage.

name: Body Control Obstacle Course
desc: Players navigate a course of bags, cones, hurdles, and a ladder — simulating broken-play scenarios in a compact space. Tests body control, balance, and the ability to adjust on the fly.

name: Footwork Mirror with Signal
desc: Two players mirror each other with the leader adding directional signals — point left, right, forward, back — and the follower executes the correct footwork immediately. Adds cognitive load to agility.

name: Leverage Hold Competition
desc: Two players lock up in a double-hand hold and compete to gain ground for 5 seconds. Teaches leverage, base width, and the ability to maintain ground in hand-to-hand contact.

name: Trail Running Mechanics
desc: DBs and WRs practice trail running — maintaining the inside-hip position on a receiver running a route, staying at arm's length, and transitioning to find the ball at the last moment.

name: Gauntlet Sprint
desc: Player sprints through two rows of defenders with pool noodles or hand shields, absorbing contact without slowing down. Reinforces contact balance and the ability to run through pressure.

name: Route Stem Footwork
desc: WRs walk through the footwork stem of each route — the specific foot pattern at the top of the route before the break. Precision stems make routes look identical until the break point.

name: QB Drop Footwork Isolation
desc: QB works 3, 5, and 7-step drops in isolation — no ball, just footwork. Coaches check the first step quickness, body lean, and platform timing at the top of the drop.

name: Zone Coverage Pedal & Find
desc: Defenders backpedal or shuffle into their zone, find all receivers in their area, and break on the first one targeted by the QB. Develops zone coverage awareness and ball-finding instincts.

name: Approach & Tackle Angle
desc: Defenders run to specific tackle angles — cutback, sideline, middle — and break down before contact on a ball carrier dummy. Correct angle is confirmed by the coach before the rep counts.

name: Competitive Sprint-Off
desc: Two players race side by side off the line. First to 20 yards wins. Competitive sprint reps build first-step urgency and push players to full effort in a simple, measurable format.

name: Individual Technique Review
desc: Coach works one-on-one with each player on a specific technique identified from film — hand punch timing, step angle, or stance width. Individual attention accelerates development across the roster.

name: Cover 4 Two-High Read
desc: QB reads the two-high safety shell of Cover 4 — identify the quarters coverage, attack the boundary corner on a deep out, or hit the post over the safety rotation. Full 7-on-7 reps.

name: Nickel Pressure Package
desc: Offense runs hot routes and quick game against a nickel blitz package. QB identifies the blitz pre-snap and throws the hot route before the rusher arrives. Develop QB alertness under pressure.

name: Play-Action Deep Ball
desc: QB runs play-action to freeze the safety, then delivers a deep ball to a receiver running a post or go route behind the safeties. Work ball placement — lead the receiver and throw away from the DB.

name: Flood Concept 7-on-7
desc: Run the flood — three receivers on one side at three levels: flat, 10-yard out, and deep corner. QB reads the corner: if he takes the flat, throw the out; if he takes the out, throw the corner.

name: Switch & Trade Releases
desc: Two adjacent receivers run switch routes — crossing before the line of scrimmage to swap release lanes. Focus on the depth of the cross, timing of the mesh, and QB delivery to the new alignment.

name: Speed-Out Timing Drill
desc: WR aligns in the slot, runs a 3-step speed out, and catches the ball before the defender can rotate. QB delivers on the WR's third step — throw before the WR comes out of the break.

name: Naked Bootleg Pass
desc: QB fakes an inside run and boots naked to the weakside. Single receiver runs a post-wheel or drag. QB must scan across his body and deliver an accurate ball while on the move.

name: Spot Route Concept
desc: Install the spot concept — three receivers run converging routes to the same area, creating a rub. QB reads the linebacker coverage — whoever is late gets the ball. Precision timing required.

name: Double Post High-Low
desc: Run double posts — inside and outside — creating a high-low read on the free safety. If he sinks on the inside post, throw the outside; if he hangs, hit the inside seam.

name: Out-and-Up Double Move
desc: WR runs an out route to freeze the corner, then breaks vertical up the sideline. QB holds the safety with his eyes and throws the go on the WR's second break. Sells the fake on every rep.

name: Shallow Cross Concept
desc: Install the shallow cross — one receiver runs a 4-yard crossing route while a second runs a deeper crosser behind. QB reads the linebacker drops and hits the open crossing route.

name: TE Seam Conversion
desc: TE works the seam against linebacker coverage — split the safeties in Cover 2, run past the hook-curl defender in Cover 3. QB delivers on the TE's third step on any vertical threat.

name: Tunnel Screen Installation
desc: Install the tunnel screen — interior linemen step back and release into the tunnel, QB throws to the receiver catching behind the line, and the blockers form the wall. Timing is everything.

name: Jet Motion Pass Attack
desc: WR jets in motion pre-snap. QB reads the defense's adjustment — if the defense runs with him, throw behind the motion; if they stay, attack the vacated space. Versatile motion-based attack.

name: Half-Field 7-on-7
desc: Offense works the strong or weak side of the formation only. QB focuses on reading one side of the field — simplifies the progression read and develops side-specific route combinations.

name: Hitch-and-Go Route
desc: WR runs a 5-yard hitch to sell the curl, then breaks vertical off the corner's break. QB reads the corner's leverage and throws the go if the corner commits to the hitch.

name: Levels Concept 7-on-7
desc: Install the levels concept — receiver at 5 yards, 10 yards, and 15 yards on the same side. QB reads the linebacker drops and hits the level that has the most space.

name: Stacked WR Release
desc: Two WRs align in a stack formation and run complementary routes — one breaks inside, one breaks outside. Focus on the horizontal stretch they create and the QB's quick read.

name: QB Footwork Under Pressure
desc: Coach applies mild pressure from behind as QB drops. QB must reset feet, step through the throw, and deliver accurately under simulated pocket disruption. Builds pocket composure.

name: Slant-Flat Combination
desc: WR runs a slant and RB or TE runs a flat route simultaneously. QB reads the inside linebacker — if he takes the slant, throw the flat; if he widens, fire the slant on the break.

name: Deep Ball Accuracy Drill
desc: QB throws 40-50 yard deep balls to a receiver running a go route — targeting the back shoulder at the pylon or between the numbers. Track accuracy percentage over 10 reps per session.

name: Pre-Snap Protection Calls
desc: QB and O-line run through protection calls against various defensive fronts — identify the Mike, call the protection slide, and adjust the backs' check responsibilities. No snap, all communication.

name: Y-Cross Concept
desc: TE runs a deep cross at 12-15 yards while WRs clear the space. QB reads the middle linebacker then delivers to the TE crossing the field. High-percentage big-gain play when well-timed.

name: Quick Screen to the Boundary
desc: WR aligns on the boundary and catches a quick screen with the QB catching the snap and throwing immediately. WR must make the catch and block the corner simultaneously.

name: 7-on-7 Situational Script
desc: Run a full scripted 7-on-7 session — first and ten, third and medium, red zone, two-minute. Track completion percentage and score outcomes. Film the full session for review.

name: Back-Shoulder Throw Mechanics
desc: QB and WR work the back-shoulder throw in isolation — WR sells vertical, QB leads toward the sideline, WR adjusts back. Teaches spatial anticipation and ball placement away from the DB.

name: Four Verticals Spacing Read
desc: QB works the spacing reads within four verticals — identify which safety is two-high, find the seam behind the inside linebacker, and deliver the ball on time to the open receiver.

name: Pick Route Concept Install
desc: Install legal pick or rub routes — receiver runs a route that creates a natural traffic conflict for the defender on the second receiver. Focus on exact route depths and crossing timing.

name: Compressed Bunch Formation
desc: Run routes out of a compressed bunch set — trips, stack, and cluster formations. Defense must rotate to handle the alignment; QB reads the coverage rotation and finds the open receiver.

name: QB Eyes & Manipulate Safety
desc: QB works eye manipulation — look off the deep safety to one side, then deliver to the opposite receiver. Practiced against a live safety. Develops the anticipation and deception needed to attack over the top.

name: Completion Percentage Circuit
desc: QB throws 5 reps each of: 5-yard out, 12-yard curl, 20-yard post, screen, and red zone fade. Track completion percentage across 25 throws. Weekly comparison builds accuracy accountability.

name: Motion & Shift Adjustment
desc: Offense shifts formation or sends a player in motion pre-snap. QB reads the defensive adjustment — identify leverage change, communicate the new protection, and confirm the route adjustment.

name: Trips Formation Attack
desc: Offense runs a full script of trips-formation pass plays — smash, shallow cross, and drive concept. Defense must rotate to handle the trips; QB reads the rotating coverage and attacks the void.

name: RPO Throw Decision
desc: QB reads a conflict defender post-snap on a run-pass option — give on inside zone, pull and throw a quick slant or bubble. Decision must be made in under 0.5 seconds. Timed and tracked.

name: Throw on the Run
desc: QB rolls left and right and throws to moving targets — a receiver running a crossing or out route in the direction of the QB's movement. Focus on setting the base and generating arm velocity on the run.

name: Quick Hitch Install
desc: WRs run the hitch route at 5 yards — drive off the line at full speed, stop on the outside foot, and snap back to the QB. Ball is thrown as the WR's foot hits the ground on the break.

name: Third-Down Conversion Script
desc: Offense runs 10 third-down situations: 3rd and 2, 3rd and 5, 3rd and 8, 3rd and 12. Specific route packages for each situation. Track conversions. Defense plays the appropriate coverage per down.

name: Tight-Window Throw Drill
desc: QB throws through a narrow window defined by two defenders holding shields. Receiver catches on the other side. Forces the QB to trust his footwork, throw with anticipation, and drive the ball with velocity.

name: Release & Separation Timing
desc: WR works a full release sequence off the line — head fake, hard stem, then break — against a corner. QB delivers on the break. Develops the precision timing between release, route, and throw.

name: Double Move Corner Route
desc: WR runs an in-breaking route to freeze the safety, then breaks back to the corner. QB reads the safety rotation and delivers the corner route before the safety can recover.

name: Empty Formation 7-on-7
desc: Offense runs from an empty backfield — five WRs, no RB. QB reads full-field from the snap with no run threat. Defense must account for every receiver. High-value pass attack reps.

name: Fade vs. Bracket Coverage
desc: WR runs a fade against a corner and safety playing bracket coverage. QB must deliver to the back pylon with precision — too much space and the safety arrives; too little and the corner undercuts.

name: Bunch Stack Release Patterns
desc: Three receivers in a bunch set work their individual release patterns — outside, inside, and seam — in coordination. Focus on the release timing that creates conflict for the defense.

name: Quick-Game Versus Blitz
desc: Offense runs their full quick game menu against a blitz-heavy defense — hot routes, sight adjustments, and automatic checks. QB must get the ball out in under 2 seconds on every rep.

name: Deep Out Route Mechanics
desc: WR works the deep out at 14-16 yards — run at full speed, plant the inside foot hard, and snap out to the sideline. QB must throw with lead placement 1 yard toward the sideline.

name: Tight End Route Running
desc: TEs practice their full route tree — seam, drag, arrow, corner, and wheel — in isolation with a QB. Focus on the TE's release off the line, transition from blocking stance to route running.

name: Two-Man Route Combinations
desc: Two receivers work complementary routes — smash, curl-flat, and whip combinations — in isolation with the QB. Develop the route timing and spacing that makes these combinations effective.

name: WR Alignment Adjustments
desc: WRs practice aligning correctly based on formation tags — nub, twins, trips, stacked, bunch, compressed. Correct alignment is the first job; routes only work from the right starting point.

name: Pressure to Movement Drill
desc: Scout rushers simulate interior pressure. QB steps up in the pocket, resets his base, and delivers to a receiver over the middle. Builds the pocket awareness to move within structure.

name: Seam Read vs. Cover 2
desc: QB reads the Cover 2 seam — identify the overhang safety, throw the inside seam route behind him, or check down to the flat when the safety rotates. Classic Cover 2 attack drill.

name: Zone-Beater Crossing Routes
desc: Run zone-beating crossers — high-low crossers, drive concept, and mesh — against a Cover 3 shell. WRs find the void between the hook-curl and flat defenders and sit down in the soft spot.

name: QB Catch & Throw
desc: QB catches a snap from the ground (like a shotgun snap) and immediately sets feet and throws to a moving target. Develops the snap-to-throw transition time and the ability to process on the catch.

name: Compressed Red Zone Routes
desc: WRs and TEs work routes inside the 10 with compressed alignments — no room to stem, release is tighter, and every route has a 3-yard variation from normal depth. QB must adjust ball placement.

name: Third-and-Long Package
desc: Install the third-and-long package — deep crossers, digs, and combination routes designed to gain 10+ yards against zone coverage. Defense plays prevent; QB works the intermediate windows.

name: Two QB Competition Drill
desc: Two QBs alternate throws to the same receiver. Each throw is graded — completion, ball placement, and timing. Competitive format sharpens both QBs and clearly identifies the better thrower on each concept.

name: Snag Concept Install
desc: Run the snag concept — corner route, snag (5-yard break toward the sideline), and a flat route creating a three-level stretch to one side. QB reads the corner then checks the snag.

name: Comeback Route Technique
desc: WR runs full-speed vertically for 14-16 yards then snaps back to the QB — no drift on the break. QB must throw before the WR comes out of the break. Develops the intermediate passing game.

name: Route Combination Library
desc: Walk-through session installing 5 new route combinations from the playbook — no live defenders, just precise route depth and spacing. QB confirms the right receiver on each concept before adding defense.

name: Full Playbook 7-on-7
desc: Unrestricted 7-on-7 drawing from the full playbook — offense calls live, defense responds. Focus is on applying the full range of pass concepts against multiple coverage looks.

name: Max-Protection Check Release
desc: Run max protection with one check-release receiver. QB takes a 7-step drop, holds the ball to let the route develop, and delivers down the field. Offensive line protects a full 3.5-second pocket.

name: Draw & Dump-Off Read
desc: QB fakes a draw, reads the linebacker dropping or blitzing, and throws to the RB or TE on a check-down route. Develops the ability to use play-action to slow the linebacker and attack them in space.

name: WR Option Route
desc: WR reads the defender and breaks in the direction of most space — in or out, not predetermined. QB must read the same defender and throw to where the WR is going. Pure sight-adjustment passing.

name: Passing Game Walk-Through
desc: Full pass game walk-through at 50% speed — every WR and TE runs their exact route at the exact depth on every play in the install. Corrects alignment, release, and spacing before adding defense.

name: Screens & Outlets Circuit
desc: QB works all screen and outlet routes in sequence — RB screen, TE outlet, bubble screen, and middle screen. O-line releases rehearsed on each. Develops QB's below-the-sticks throwing confidence.

name: Backfield Route Releases
desc: RBs and FBs work all their route releases from the backfield — flat, angle, wheel, check-down, and swing. QB coordinates timing with each release. Develops the backs as a true receiving dimension.

name: Vertical Stretch Read
desc: Run a four-vertical concept with a built-in high-low read — inside seam paired with a check-down underneath. QB reads the deep safety and either attacks the seam or dumps to the back.

name: Wide Receiver Screen
desc: WR aligns wide, the QB fakes a dropback and throws immediately to the WR catching behind the line. Two interior linemen release to block. WR reads the blocks and accelerates upfield.

name: Quick Passing Accuracy Drill
desc: QB throws 5 routes in sequence without stopping — slant, out, hitch, seam, flat — against air. Focus on footwork reset between throws, shoulder turn, and consistent release point.

name: Playaction Boot vs. Cover 3
desc: QB runs a play-action bootleg against a Cover 3 shell — freeze the flat defender, read the corner backpedaling, and hit the seam or corner route behind the corner's drop.

name: Deep Cross Versus Two-High
desc: TE or slot receiver runs a deep cross at 15 yards against a two-high safety shell. QB delivers between the two safeties who can't rotate fast enough to close. Timing and ball placement are critical.

name: Bubble Screen Attack
desc: Slot receiver runs a bubble screen while two outside WRs block their corner and safety assignments. QB reads the numbers advantage pre-snap before throwing the bubble.

name: Post-Snap DB Reads
desc: QB works reading the post-snap movements of DBs — if the corner opens his hips inside, throw the fade; if the safety rolls to the strong side, attack the weak-side seam. Develops advanced QB IQ.

name: Goal-Line Fade-Stop Combo
desc: WR runs a fade while the slot runs a stop route in the back of the end zone. QB reads the corner coverage — if playing the fade, throw the stop; if sagging on the stop, loft the fade.

name: Bunch Pass Concepts
desc: Three receivers in a bunch set run the corner-flat, switch, and smash combination. Defense must choose which receiver to cover; QB reads that choice and delivers to the uncovered receiver.

name: Full Team Passing Period
desc: Full team passing period — all positions aligned, full protections, full route combinations versus full 11-man defense. Every position group runs their assignments on every play. Final passing evaluation before game week.

name: Trap Play Install
desc: Install the trap — guard pulls backside and traps the penetrating defensive tackle who is left unblocked at the snap. RB reads the trap block and hits the open crease hard. Work both sides.

name: Pin-and-Pull Sweep
desc: Tackle pins the defensive end inside while the guard pulls around to kick out the corner. RB follows the pulling guard around the edge. Develop the pull path and pin-block technique together.

name: Speed Option
desc: QB sprints the edge on a speed option — pitch key is the cornerback. If the corner attacks the QB, he pitches to the RB. If the corner hangs on the pitch, QB keeps. Live pitch decisions.

name: Wham Block Install
desc: TE or H-back kicks in on a wham block — sneaking behind the line and hitting a down defensive lineman who is being left unblocked by the offensive line. Timing between the wham blocker and RB path.

name: Iso Play Fundamentals
desc: Install the isolation play — fullback leads through the hole and blocks the linebacker while the RB follows directly into the open gap. FB must take the proper path and block at the second level.

name: RB Pass Protection
desc: RBs work their pass protection assignments — identify the free blitzer, set their protection angle, and absorb the hit with proper base and technique. Critical for keeping the QB upright.

name: Stretch to Cutback Read
desc: RB runs the outside zone stretch and reads the backside defensive end — if the end closes hard, cut back across his face. If the end stays, continue outside. Develop the patience and vision needed.

name: FB Lead Blocking
desc: Fullbacks work their lead-blocking assignments in isolation — identifying the linebacker to block, taking the correct path, and delivering a shoulder block at the second level.

name: Off-Tackle Power
desc: Install the off-tackle power run — tackle kicks out the defensive end, guard leads inside to the linebacker, and the RB runs the B or C gap off the kick-out block.

name: Cut-Block Technique
desc: Offensive linemen work cut blocks on moving defenders — approach low, drive the shoulder into the defender's thighs, and drive through on the contact. Used in zone blocking to slow pursuit.

name: Long Trap & Middle Trap
desc: Guard pulls across two gaps for a long trap or one gap for a middle trap. Develop the different pull paths, angles to the trap target, and the timing of the handoff to the RB.

name: Downhill North-South Running
desc: RB takes handoffs and runs directly north-south through the line with no hesitation. Place bags in gaps to simulate holes. Develop the instinct to press the line before cutting.

name: Perimeter Blocking Scheme
desc: WRs and TEs work perimeter blocks on outside runs — crack block, stalk block, and seal block on corners and safeties. Perimeter blocking execution determines the success of any edge run.

name: Zone Read RPO Run
desc: Full offense runs zone-read plays with attached RPO bubble or slant. QB decides to hand off, pull, or throw based on the read key. Live reps with scout defense making decisions.

name: QB Designed Keeper
desc: QB executes pre-designed keepers — the blocking is set up specifically for the QB to keep and run a gap. Work QB's pad level, vision, and contact balance on the designed run.

name: Gap Scheme Combination
desc: Run all gap scheme plays in sequence — power, counter, trap, and sweep — against a live defense. Film and compare execution. Develops familiarity with each scheme's timing and blocking angles.

name: RB Vision & Patience Drill
desc: RB takes a handoff and runs behind a coach holding a large pad who moves through various gaps in slow motion. RB must read the coach's movement and cut off the correct block.

name: Wildcat Formation Runs
desc: Snap directly to the RB or a second QB in the Wildcat. Install the read option, speed sweep, and dive from the Wildcat formation. Defense must adjust to a non-traditional ball handler.

name: Pin-Block vs. Backside Pursuit
desc: Backside offensive linemen work pin blocks to seal off backside pursuit on outside zone runs. If the RB cuts back, he needs a clean lane — develop the pin-block technique needed to provide it.

name: Short-Yardage Run Package
desc: Install short-yardage runs — QB sneak, dive, and power from a jumbo formation. Run from the 1, 2, and 3-yard line. Defense plays live. Result tracked for conversion percentage.

name: Counter Crack Sweep
desc: WR cracks the safety inside while a guard pulls to the edge and blocks the corner. RB takes a counter step and cuts to the crack-crack gap. Full coordination between the crack block and pull timing.

name: Running Back Receiving Routes
desc: RBs work their receiving routes — flat, angle, wheel, swing, and seam — in a dedicated 7-on-7 period. Develops the RB as a pass-catching weapon and improves their route-running precision.

name: Speed Sweep Execution
desc: Fast athlete — RB, slot WR, or QB — runs a speed sweep with a guard leading around the edge. Timing of the handoff, path of the runner, and lead block angle practiced at full speed.

name: RB & FB Double-Team Block
desc: RB and FB work a double-team block on the edge — one kicks out, one leads through the hole. Develops the timing and coordination of backfield tandem blocking used in power and iso schemes.

name: OL Run Block Evaluation
desc: O-line runs three consecutive zone blocking reps against a live scout defense while a coach grades every individual rep — first step, reach angle, and finish block.

name: Power Read Install
desc: Install power read — a traditional power play with the QB reading the defensive end who is left unblocked. If the end closes on the RB, QB pulls and keeps outside.

name: Jet Sweep Motion Attack
desc: WR runs full speed in jet motion and takes a handoff or a direct snap on the sweep. All blockers adjust to jet motion responsibilities. Timing of the pitch or hand-off in motion is critical.

name: Run Blocking Finish
desc: Linemen complete their run blocks — drive the defender 5 yards, maintain contact through the whistle, and finish. Too many drills stop at contact; this drill demands a complete block through the whistle.

name: Backside Cutback Lane
desc: RB reads the frontside run being sealed off and cuts to the backside lane. Backside linemen work their pin blocks. Develops the patience and vision to find the cutback in zone-heavy defenses.

name: Run Fit Adjustment Drill
desc: Defense adjusts their run fits based on the offensive formation — base 4-3, light box, heavy formation. Offense shows each formation; defense communicates and adjusts their gaps before the snap.

name: Half-Line Run Drill
desc: Half the O-line and half the D-line work isolated run-fit and run-block reps on one side of the center. Allows more reps per player and isolates individual technique within the scheme.

name: RB Stiff Arm Technique
desc: RB works the stiff arm in isolation — plant the hand on the defender's helmet or shoulder pad and extend fully. Work the lead-hand stiff arm and the trail-hand stiff arm from both sides.

name: Ball Security Contact Drill
desc: RB carries the ball through a contact gauntlet — each station attempts to strip the ball while the RB maintains four-point contact and drives forward. Ball security is the only metric.

name: RB Juke in Space
desc: RB receives a hand-off and faces a live defender in open space — juke, spin, or hurdle to avoid the tackle. No lanes, just space and one-on-one. Develops the open-field elusiveness needed for big plays.

name: Counter-Jet Combination
desc: Combine the counter run and jet sweep off the same motion. The defense must honor both threats. QB reads which defender commits to the jet and attacks the opposite gap with the counter.

name: Full Run-Game Script
desc: Run a full scripted 11-on-11 run-game period — 15 plays covering all runs in the game plan. Defense plays live. Film every rep. Grades on per-play success: positive gain defined as 4+ yards.

name: Third-and-Short Run Package
desc: Install specific third-and-short runs — QB sneak, power, and trap draw. Defense plays their third-and-short package live. Converts must feel routine through repetition, not surprise.

name: Play-Action Run Fake
desc: QB takes the snap, sells a convincing run fake to the RB, and sets up for a pass. Focus on the QB's mesh depth, eye contact with the run key, and the transition from fake to pass platform.

name: Zone Left & Zone Right
desc: Run inside zone left and right alternately against a live defense. OL must recognize the playside and backside on each call and adjust their steps accordingly. No thinking — react and execute.

name: Trap vs. 3-Technique
desc: Specifically work the trap against a 3-technique defensive tackle — the hardest trap assignment. Guard must pull and reach the DT before he penetrates. Timed and evaluated on hit-rate.

name: Fullback Dive
desc: FB runs the dive play between the guards — direct snap or handoff, north-south at maximum effort. Defense plays a full box. Simple but explosive play practiced until the FB can hit it without hesitation.

name: RB Reading the Mike
desc: RB works with a coach holding a pad representing the middle linebacker. RB takes the handoff and cuts away from wherever the coach moves the pad. Develops the instinct to cut off the linebacker.

name: Shotgun Draw Install
desc: QB receives shotgun snap, delays 1.5 seconds simulating a pass look, and hands to the RB on a draw path. OL sets up as pass protection before releasing to blocks at the second level.

name: G-Power vs. 4-3 Under
desc: Run G-Power (guard pull power) specifically against a 4-3 Under front. OL identifies the kick-out target, the pulling guard takes the correct path, and the RB presses the hole before cutting.

name: Reverse & Flea Flicker
desc: Install the reverse — end-around handoff to a WR — and the flea flicker off the reverse. Full-team execution with defense running live. Sells the misdirection and develops timing.

name: Compression Run vs. Light Box
desc: Run inside zone against a light box — 5 or 6 men in the box. OL must block with efficiency; RB must press the A-gap and hit the crease before the secondary fills.

name: RB Chip & Release Route
desc: RB chips the edge rusher on the snap — one hard hit — then releases on a route in the flat or angle. QB reads the RB as the check-down after the chip. Combines run blocking and receiving skill.

name: Run-Game Install Period
desc: Dedicated run-game install period — new plays installed at walk speed, then half speed, then full live reps. Every lineman, back, and tight end confirmed on their assignment before moving on.

name: Split-Zone with TE Kick
desc: Install split-zone — inside zone play where a TE or H-back kicks out the backside defensive end opposite the zone direction. Creates a cutback crease for the RB without a pulling lineman.

name: Veer Option Install
desc: Install the veer — QB reads the defensive tackle who is unblocked at the snap. If the DT closes, hand off to the dive back; if he widens, QB keeps outside and optionally pitches to the pitchman.

name: Inverted Veer
